basics.name: Yu Hon, ON (Henry)
basics.label: Machine Learning Researcher and Engineer | Healthcare AI | Computer Vision
basics.summary: Machine Learning Researcher and Engineer trained at Imperial College London, specialising in healthcare AI and computer vision. Experienced in building end-to-end machine learning pipelines for imaging and clinical prediction, from data curation and preprocessing to model training, validation, and evaluation on HPC systems. No sponsorship required.

work.name: Imperial College London
work.summary: Research and teaching role involving biomedical image analysis, feature extraction, machine learning, and support for computer vision/image processing practicals.
highlights: Built scalable feature extraction pipelines for high-throughput microscopy data using PyFeats, enabling reproducible downstream machine learning analysis across 24 samples/images/experiments.

Engineered and benchmarked classical machine learning models in scikit-learn with structured preprocessing, feature selection, and automated reporting, improving experiment comparison and reducing manual analysis effort by 80%.
Supported 100+ students in computer vision and image processing practicals, debugging, optimising, and validating Python pipelines to improve code correctness and experimental reliability.

work.name: Sun Yat-sen University Cancer Centre
work.summary: Research role involving multimodal biomedical data, clinical prediction modelling, oncology imaging, and radiotherapy-related workflows.
highlights: Developed statistical and CNN-based prediction models in Python using Keras and scikit-learn with multimodal omics and 3D medical imaging data from 180+ cases, supporting robust biomedical analysis.

Supported 500+ dosimetry planning and radiotherapy QA workflows using Monaco, Eclipse, and Pinnacle, collaborating with clinicians and physicists in a regulated clinical environment to ensure accurate and compliant delivery.
Worked within clinical documentation and validation standards, strengthening experience in stakeholder-driven analysis and healthcare data governance.

work.name: Viridien
work.summary: Technical role involving large-scale signal processing, numerical modelling, imaging datasets, and client-facing analytical outputs.
highlights: Applied large-scale signal processing and numerical modelling to complex imaging datasets across 3 projects, delivering analytical outputs for technical and client-facing use.
Translated modelling results into clear recommendations for clients, supporting data-driven interpretation and decision-making.
Collaborated with R&D teams to enhance internal seismic visualisation tools, reducing workflow complexity by 50% by consolidating a two-tool presentation process into a single standardised workflow.
Designed and led end-to-end machine learning research pipelines for novel cardiac MRI analysis, spanning data ingestion, preprocessing, training, validation, inference, and quantitative evaluation.
Conducted large-scale experimentation on datasets ranging from hundreds to thousands of labelled scans, including 10,000+ cases for self-supervised model pre-training.

Collaborated closely with clinicians and academic stakeholders to define clinically meaningful endpoints, interpret model outputs, and translate research findings into actionable insights, contributing to peer-reviewed publications and challenge submissions.
Implemented automated evaluation pipelines with quantitative metrics and diagnostic visualisations, including ROC/PR analysis, to support rigorous model comparison and failure-mode analysis.
Advanced training in medical imaging, signal processing, quantitative data analysis, and regulatory considerations for medical technologies.
Developed strong foundations in interdisciplinary problem-solving across engineering, computation, and healthcare applications.
Built strong technical foundations in signal processing, computer vision, numerical methods, and scientific programming in Python and MATLAB.
Developed core analytical and engineering skills relevant to machine learning, imaging, and data-driven modelling.

publications.name: Classification of Mitral Regurgitation from Cardiac Cine MRI using Clinically-Interpretable Morphological Features
publications.summary: Publication/presentation in medical imaging venues focusing on mitral regurgitation classification using clinically interpretable cardiac morphology features.

publications.name: Two-Stage nnU-Net for Automatic Multi-Class Bi-Atrial Segmentation from LGE-MRIs
publications.summary: Challenge submission focused on automatic multi-class bi-atrial segmentation from LGE-MRI using a two-stage nnU-Net approach.

publications.name: Automatic Aortic Valve Pathology Detection from 3-Chamber Cine MRI with Spatio-Temporal Attention Maps
publications.summary: Research output on automatic aortic valve pathology detection from 3-chamber cine MRI with spatio-temporal attention maps.

publications.name: Automatic Aortic Valve Pathology Detection using 3-Chamber Cine MRI
publications.summary: Poster presentation on automatic aortic valve pathology detection using 3-chamber cine MRI.

publications.name: Neural Network Tools for Automatic Identification of Abnormalities in Late Gadolinium-Enhanced Cardiac MRI
publications.summary: Poster presentation on neural network tools for automatic identification of abnormalities in late gadolinium-enhanced cardiac MRI.

awards.title: 2nd Place, Multi-Class Bi-Atrial Segmentation Challenge
awards.summary: Awarded 2nd place in the Multi-Class Bi-Atrial Segmentation Challenge at MICCAI 2024.

projects.name: End-to-End Cardiac MRI Machine Learning Pipelines
projects.description: PhD research developing reproducible machine learning pipelines for cardiac MRI analysis, spanning data ingestion, preprocessing, model training, validation, inference, and quantitative evaluation.
highlights: Applied deep learning to cardiac MRI segmentation and disease classification.
Implemented automated evaluation pipelines with quantitative metrics and diagnostic visualisations.
Conducted experiments across labelled datasets ranging from hundreds to thousands of scans and 10,000+ cases for self-supervised pre-training.

projects.name: Mitral Regurgitation Classification with Morphological Features
projects.description: Machine learning project using clinically interpretable cardiac morphology features extracted from cine MRI for mitral regurgitation classification.
highlights: Used feature engineering and classical machine learning for interpretable cardiac disease classification.
Documented the workflow as a reproducible portfolio project.

projects.name: High-Throughput Microscopy Feature Extraction
projects.description: Research Assistant project building scalable feature extraction pipelines for high-throughput microscopy data using PyFeats.
highlights: Enabled reproducible downstream machine learning analysis across 24 samples/images/experiments.
Reduced manual analysis effort through structured preprocessing, feature selection, and automated reporting.
